nodes.label: AI as collaborative creative partner for student engagement
nodes.label_negative: AI as non-interactive or non-creative tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to create images collaboratively with students to then use a writing stimuli

nodes.label: AI as personal knowledge trainer for unfamiliar topics
nodes.grounding_span: I also use it to train myself on unfamiliar topics, particularly maths

nodes.label: AI as source of clear examples and clarifying questions
nodes.grounding_span: having really clear examples of maths concepts and the ability to ask clarifying questions

nodes.label: AI for quick and easy tasks vs. personal preference for self-handling
nodes.label_negative: AI for tasks I find quick and easy to do myself
nodes.grounding_span: I never ask AI to help me to write emails or messages because I find it quick and easy to do this myself

nodes.label: AI as affordable alternative to human tutoring
nodes.label_negative: Human tutoring as more expensive but personalized
nodes.grounding_span: less demand for tuition as AI options grow in popularity, it's a much more affordable option

nodes.label: Student engagement and investment in learning
nodes.grounding_span: makes them really invested in the writing

nodes.label: Teaching confidence and competence
nodes.grounding_span: gives me confidence that I am not missing anything and can effectively teach the concept

nodes.label: Efficiency and time management
nodes.grounding_span: time is always a factor in an hour long session

nodes.label: Affordable access to quality education
nodes.grounding_span: it's a much more affordable option

nodes.label: Enthusiasm for AI's creative collaborative use with students
nodes.grounding_span: It's great fun for them, makes them really invested in the writing

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for building teaching confidence
nodes.grounding_span: gives me confidence that I am not missing anything

nodes.label: Resistance to using AI for quick personal tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I never ask AI to help me to write emails or messages

nodes.label: Forward-looking acceptance of AI's impact on tutoring demand
nodes.grounding_span: less demand for tuition as AI options grow in popularity

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative refinement — works through examples and questions
nodes.grounding_span: I might ask how to do something, process the information, ask for examples, etc. Then I'll work through questions myself

nodes.label: Habitual reliance on traditional methods despite awareness of AI
nodes.grounding_span: old habits die hard and I often just trawl through resources instead
